panels.description: Uses `absent_over_time` on `sonarqube_web_uptime_minutes` to detect availability. Returns **Down** (`1`) when no pod has reported the metric in the last minute. Returns no data (null) when at least one pod is reporting — null is mapped to **OK**.
panels.title: Is SQS Up

options.content:
# SonarQube Server Resources

- 🗺️ [How to use this dashboard?](https://github.com/sonar-solutions/sonarqube-server-monitoring-guide/blob/main/dashboards/sonarqube-grafana-prometheus-k8s/how-to-use-this-dashboard.md)
- 📖 [SonarQube Server Monitoring Guide](https://github.com/sonar-solutions/sonarqube-server-monitoring-guide)
- 📄 [SonarQube Server Documentation](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server)
- 🎧 [Sonar Help Center](https://help.sonarsource.com/) (Knowledge base, Support portal)
- 🎓 [Sonar Learning Center](https://www.sonarsource.com/learn)
- 🆕 [What's new in SonarQube Server](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarqube/whats-new/)
- 💬 [Sonar Community Forum](https://community.sonarsource.com/)

---

🙏 Thank you for using this dashboard!\
❓ Any questions, issues, or suggestions? Please open an issue on [GitHub]().

panels.description: Number of background task errors in the selected time range.
panels.title: Background tasks in the last 24h

panels.description: Number of successful and failed background tasks **in the selected time range.**
panels.title: Tasks in time range

panels.description: At any point in the chart, the value is **the number of successfully completed Compute Engine tasks in the preceding hour.** This is a measure of analysis-related traffic on the server. For example, it allows detecting analysis peak times.
panels.title: Successful task throughput

panels.description: How many background tasks are being processed by the compute engine?
panels.title: In progress background tasks

panels.description: Each pod (node) is reporting the value for all nodes at the time the metric was updated. As the update is not in synch across pods, **the values might differ slightly.** However, the absolute values are not as important as the general trend, so it is the average that matters. What is the average value? For how long is it increased?
panels.title: Pending background tasks

panels.description: Each pod (node) is reporting the value for all nodes at the time the metric was updated. As the update is not in synch across pods, **the values might differ slightly.** However, the absolute values are not as important as the general trend, so it is the average that matters. What is the average value? For how long is it increased?
panels.title: Max background task pending time

panels.description:
DevOps platform integration status indicates whether the connections from SonarQube Server to the DevOps platforms are healthy.

**If any connection is in an error state, the status for that integration is error.**

**Note:** that error is also returned if the integration is not configured, so it is recommended to remove the irrelevant metrics.
panels.title: DevOps Integrations

panels.description:
If the status is Error, it means **SonarQube Elastic Search status is RED**. Log review is needed to investigate RED status.

Investigate in this order:
1. es.log
2. ce.log
3. web.log
panels.title: ElasticSearch

panels.description: ElasticSearch storage utilization for each pod. Utilization over 60% is already a warning as the storage size should be increased before reaching 85%.
panels.title: ElasticSearch storage utilization

panels.description:
How much data is stored on storage disk(s) used by ElasticSearch.

This doesn't represent the size of the index, but the size of the file system. If persistence is enabled, the amount of data roughly corresponds to the index size.
panels.title: ElasticSearch: storage used
SonarQube web and compute engine JVM metrics

panels.description: Tracks how long Prometheus takes to scrape metrics from each SonarQube endpoint. Under normal conditions scrape time is low and stable. When the application is under heavy load or experiencing degraded performance, scrape time can increase significantly — making it **a useful early indicator that something is wrong.**
panels.title: Scrape duration

options.content:
Monitors pod health, replica counts, CPU, and memory
for the SonarQube workloads running in the cluster.

**Enterprise Edition (EE):** Runs as a single pod
(StatefulSet). All metrics appear in the StatefulSet
charts only; Deployment charts show no data.

**Data Center Edition (DCE):** Runs as two workloads —
Application pods (Deployment) handling web/CE traffic,
and Search pods (StatefulSet) running Elasticsearch.
Each has its own CPU and memory charts.

Check **desired vs ready difference** first when
investigating availability issues — any non-zero value
means pods are missing.

targets.expr:
max(
  (time() - kube_pod_start_time{namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*sonarqube.*", pod!~".*-search-.*"})
  * on(pod, namespace) group_left()
  (kube_pod_status_phase{namespace="$namespace", pod=~".*sonarqube.*", pod!~".*-search-.*", phase="Running"} == 1)
)

panels.description:
Enteprise Edition: The SonarQube pod (only 1)

Data Center Edition: search pods
panels.title: StatefulSet pod replicas

panels.description: Compare required vs ready pod to see if/when there might have been an issue.
panels.title: SonarQube replicas: required and ready

panels.description:
SonarQube EE: The CPU used by the single SonarQube pod

SonarQube DCE: CPU utilization in Search pods
panels.title: StatefulSet CPU core utilization

panels.description: SonarQube DCE: CPU utilization in Application pods
panels.title: Deployment CPU utilization (DCE only: application pods)

panels.description:
SonarQube EE: The memory used by the single SonarQube pod

SonarQube DCE: memory utilization in Search pods
panels.title: StatefulSet memory utilization

panels.description: SonarQube DCE: memory utilization in Application pods
panels.title: Deployment memory utilization (DCE only: application pods)

options.content:
### Database Monitoring

SonarQube's performance is directly tied to the health of its database.
Resource exhaustion (CPU, memory), slow disk I/O, poor configuration,
connection pool saturation, or neglected maintenance (outdated
statistics, fragmented indexes) will all manifest as application-level
degradation — **monitoring SonarQube alone is not sufficient.**

Because database monitoring depends on the specific database engine
and the infrastructure it runs on, no database visualizations are
included in this dashboard. Extend this dashboard or use a dedicated
database monitoring solution alongside it to cover this critical
component.
